programme.title: Confucius: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes visits China on the trail of Confucius, a great sage of Chinese history.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: A journey to the Lambeyeque valley in Peru and the ruins of 250 mysterious pyramids. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The London School of Economics faces off against University College, Oxford.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which patience and lateral thinking are as vital as knowledge, and connections need to be made between apparently unconnected things.

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates the secretive and controversial military research base.

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian takes a forensic look at the police investigation launched after Ruth's arrest. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: Stephen Poliakoff Remembers... Caught on a Train
programme.desc: Screenwriter Stephen Poliakoff looks back on his 1980 television drama Caught on a Train.

programme.title: Caught on a Train
programme.desc: A young Englishman, travelling on the Ostend to Vienna express, shares a train compartment with an American girl and an elderly Viennese lady.

programme.title: How to Get Ahead at Renaissance Court
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores Renaissance Florence under the reign of Grand Duke Cosimo Medici, where some of the greatest painters, sculptors and craftsmen in world history served.

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates the secretive and controversial military research base.

programme.title: Scotland's Finest - The Story of the Highland Games
programme.desc: Bill Patterson narrates the rich and vivid story of the Highland Games, which have become a symbol of community and identity in Scotland and across the world.

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Gillian takes a forensic look at the police investigation launched after Ruth's arrest. Contains some scenes which some viewers may find upsetting.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Coventry University makes its debut appearance against a team from Cardiff University, with a place in round two at stake. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which patience and lateral thinking are as vital as knowledge, and connections need to be made between apparently unconnected things.

programme.title: Dam Busters Declassified
programme.desc: Martin Shaw separates the fact from the myth as he takes to the skies to retrace the route of the 1943 raid by 617 Squadron which used bouncing bombs to destroy German dams.

programme.title: The Great Escaper
programme.desc: WWII veteran Bernard sneaks out of his care home to attend the 70th anniversary of D-Day. Contains some strong language.

programme.title: Glenda Jackson: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie tells the story of Glenda Jackson, one of Britain's greatest-ever acting talents, who famously turned her back on her hugely successful screen career to pursue a life in politics.

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs
programme.desc: An FBI trainee shares her nightmare memory in the hope of stopping a serial killer. Contains very strong language, prolonged violence and upsetting scenes.

programme.title: Anthony Hopkins: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at the life of Oscar-winning actor Sir Anthony Hopkins, featuring interviews conducted with the BBC over the course of his career.

programme.title: Glenda Jackson: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie tells the story of Glenda Jackson, one of Britain's greatest-ever acting talents, who famously turned her back on her hugely successful screen career to pursue a life in politics.

programme.title: How to Get Ahead at Renaissance Court
programme.desc: Stephen Smith explores Renaissance Florence under the reign of Grand Duke Cosimo Medici, where some of the greatest painters, sculptors and craftsmen in world history served.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, featuring S Club 7, Pet Shop Boys, Macy Gray, Melanie C, Liam Gallagher & Steve Craddock, Eurythmics, Gabrielle and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 15 October 1999 and featuring Eurythmics, Jordan Knight, Macy Gray, Ann Lee, James and Christina Aguilera.

programme.title: TOTP: 1989
programme.desc: Andy Crane and Jenny Powell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 May 1989 and featuring Debbie Gibson, Bon Jovi, Roxette, Kylie Minogue, Bangles and Chaka Khan.

programme.title: TOTP: 1982
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, featuring the Scottish and English World Cup squads, BA Robertson, Chas & Dave, Junior, Patrice Rushen, Tight Fit and Paul McCartney & Stevie Wonder.

programme.title: Whitney at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the hits of one of the greatest and biggest-selling stars of all time that looks back at Whitney Houston's best performances at the BBC.

programme.title: Whitney Houston: Live in South Africa 1994
programme.desc: Whitney Houston's 1994 concert in Durban, South Africa, the first performance by a major western musician in the country following the end of apartheid.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, featuring S Club 7, Pet Shop Boys, Macy Gray, Melanie C, Liam Gallagher & Steve Craddock, Eurythmics, Gabrielle and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: TOTP: 1999
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 15 October 1999 and featuring Eurythmics, Jordan Knight, Macy Gray, Ann Lee, James and Christina Aguilera.

programme.title: TOTP: 1982
programme.desc: Simon Bates presents the pop chart programme, featuring the Scottish and English World Cup squads, BA Robertson, Chas & Dave, Junior, Patrice Rushen, Tight Fit and Paul McCartney & Stevie Wonder.

programme.title: TOTP 1989
programme.desc: Andy Crane and Jenny Powell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 May 1989 and featuring Debbie Gibson, Bon Jovi, Roxette, Kylie Minogue, Bangles and Chaka Khan.

programme.title: Whitney at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of the hits of one of the greatest and biggest-selling stars of all time that looks back at Whitney Houston's best performances at the BBC.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Good Old Days
programme.desc: Music hall show, filmed at the Varieties Theatre Leeds in 1976. With Edward Woodward, Larry Grayson, Frank Carson, Margaret Savage, Sheila Mathews, Peter Gale and Albert Aldred.

programme.title: Dusty
programme.desc: An edition of Dusty Springfield's 1960s variety show, first broadcast on 8 September 1966. Her special guests are American vocal quartet The Four Freshmen.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The Wainthropps' nephew has been excluded from school, but has he been wrongly accused of misbehaving? Hetty is determined to find out.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: Embedded in Parisian high society, the Count methodically destroys those who betrayed him, only to discover that vengeance threatens to consume the innocent along with the guilty.

programme.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
programme.desc: As his plans reach their end, Edmond must choose between revenge and mercy, confronting the cost of becoming the man he set out to be. Contains some violent scenes.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about an irrepressible snob. Hyacinth's quest for perfection is somewhat hampered by the dubious antics of the rest of her family.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Sitcom. Timothy's handsome cousin Brinsley comes to stay - and supplants Timothy in Mother's affections.

programme.title: Anthony Hopkins: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at the life of Oscar-winning actor Sir Anthony Hopkins, featuring interviews conducted with the BBC over the course of his career.
